analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 6 finding(s) from 7 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: Debug SBA can read boot ROM contents; no address range check observed.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write / boot ROM (i_bootrom) / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: sbaddress_o drives SBA address without range restriction
dmcontrol_d can set dmactive=1 via DMI write
i_axi_master_dm connects debug master to slave[1] (debug bus)
i_bootrom instantiated; accessible on AXI interconnect
boot ROM slave connected to master[ariane_soc::ROM], reachable via crossbar

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: debug bus master (i_axi_master_dm) Operation: read/write Object: boot ROM (i_bootrom) Expected guard: dmactive asserted, SBA enabled, address range check blocking boot ROM Observed behavior: No address-based exclusion or access check blocks debug SBA from targeting boot ROM. Debug master can set any address via sbaddress and read boot ROM data. Path: dm_csrs sbaddress → dm_sba → dm_top axi_m_req → i_axi_master_dm → AXI crossbar slave[1] → bootrom slave (ariane_soc::ROM) Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Unauthorized readout of boot firmware, potential secret leakage
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Exact address map of ROM and whether crossbar filters debug master access.

findings.summary: JTAG key is present but never enforced; DMI access is always permitted.
findings.vulnerability_category: enable DMI access / activate debug / DMI transactions (dmi_req_valid_o) / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: pass <= jtag_key; on reset only
capture_dr, shift_dr logic does not reference pass or key comparison
state machine transitions with no authentication condition

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: dmi_jtag (JTAG to DMI bridge) Operation: enable DMI access / activate debug Object: DMI transactions (dmi_req_valid_o) Expected guard: jtag_key comparison gates dmi_req_valid_o Observed behavior: jtag_key input is stored in pass register during reset, but never compared against any value, and does not gate DMI request valid. All DMI commands accepted unconditionally. Path: tck_i, tms_i, td_i → dmi_jtag → state machine → dmi_req_valid_o, no authentication step Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Full unauthenticated debug access from JTAG interface, bypassing any intended key protection

findings.summary: ndmreset can reset the full core, likely clearing any security locks.
findings.vulnerability_category: assert ndmreset_o / system reset that clears lock bits in csr_regfile / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: assign ndmreset_o = dmcontrol_q.ndmreset;
dmcontrol_d = dmi_req_i.data; ndmreset directly writable
ariane rst_ni connected to ndmreset_n, derived from ndmreset
csr_regfile uses rst_ni which is global reset, including lock bits

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: dm_csrs/dm_top (debug module) Operation: assert ndmreset_o Object: system reset that clears lock bits in csr_regfile Expected guard: ndmreset must not clear security-critical lock registers Observed behavior: dmcontrol_q.ndmreset can be set to 1 via DMI, causing ndmreset_n low, which resets the ariane core and csr_regfile completely, potentially clearing any lock bits (e.g., PMP lock). Path: dmcontrol_q.ndmreset → ndmreset_o → ndmreset_n → ariane rst_ni → csr_regfile rst_ni Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Lock bits (if present) would be erased, allowing bypass of write protection
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Actual presence and location of lock bits (e.g., PMP lock) in csr_regfile.

findings.summary: No evidence that testmode_i exposes protected state or bypasses authentication.
findings.vulnerability_category: enable test mode / internal state (DMI, scan) / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: testmode_i selects between tck_ni and tck_i for TDO clocking
testmode_i connected to dmi_jtag_tap inst
testmode_i input to dm_csrs; passed to FIFO, no other logic observed

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: testmode_i signal Operation: enable test mode Object: internal state (DMI, scan) Expected guard: testmode must not expose protected state or bypass authentication Observed behavior: testmode_i only used to select clock mux in dmi_jtag_tap (tck_n vs tck_i) and passed to FIFO; no scan chain exposure or override of authentication observed. Path: testmode_i → dmi_jtag_tap i_dft_tck_mux, dm_csrs FIFO Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: No direct security impact identified from available RTL
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full scan chain implementation not present; deeper DFT insertion might exist.

findings.summary: Cannot confirm or deny vulnerability without AES register implementation analysis.
findings.vulnerability_category: read / crypto key registers (AES) / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: i_apb_to_reg for CLINT only; no AES
i_apb_to_reg_aes present but register details not inspected

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: debug bus master (i_axi_master_dm) Operation: read Object: crypto key registers (AES) Expected guard: debug cannot read key registers Observed behavior: serpent_peripherals.sv contains no AES/key registers. ariane_peripherals.sv has i_apb_to_reg_aes but key register attributes unknown. Path: dm_axi_m_req → AXI crossbar → APB bridge → i_apb_to_reg_aes Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Potential key leakage if key registers are readable via debug master
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: AES key register access policy and address map; whether debug AXI master can reach AES slave.

findings.summary: Unclear if lock bits can be cleared via debug; requires deeper analysis of abstract command handler.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write / CPU CSRs (lock bits, performance counters) / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: CSR read path; privilege check present for some CSRs
CSR write path; condition on csr_we, no explicit debug bypass seen
command register can trigger abstract command

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: debug module abstract command / SBA Operation: read/write Object: CPU CSRs (lock bits, performance counters) Expected guard: debug writes respect lock bits Observed behavior: csr_regfile write logic does not show separate debug path, but uses csr_op_i and privilege checks. Abstract command mechanism not fully traced; debug mode might bypass privilege checks by design. Lock bit enforcement for PMP not inspected. Path: dm_csrs command → dm_top abstract cmd → hart debug mode → csr_regfile Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Potential unauthorized modification or disclosure of protected CSRs
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Abstract command execution path in dm_top; exact fu_op used during debug CSR access; PMP lock bit implementation.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
